what_it_does: Defines a namespaced CSS primitive layer for ConceptV deck surfaces, pattern backgrounds, typography, stats, lists, pills, tags, rules, watermark marks, comparison layouts, section headers, entity shape sizing, utility helpers, and resizable pane gutters.
observations: Uses a consistent cv- namespace across component, modifier, and utility classes.
Relies heavily on design tokens via CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radii, fonts, shadows, tracking, and easing.
Organizes styles into numbered sections with explanatory comments and example markup for several primitives.
Implements decorative backgrounds with gradients and pseudo-elements rather than image assets.
Includes both presentation primitives and an interaction support primitive for resizable gutters.
standouts: The file encodes deck-specific visual motifs: cream panels, gold hatching, blueprint grid, bronze labels, and gold dashed rules.
Several classes are small composable primitives with modifier variants rather than large page-specific layouts.
Pseudo-elements are used for bullets, checks, section rules, blueprint grid overlay, and gutter affordances.
conventions: BEM-like modifier naming appears in classes such as cv-panel--tight, cv-stat-num--xl, and cv-section-head--start.
All selectors are file-local in the sense of being scoped by the cv- prefix, except child selectors such as .meta and .right under .cv-panel-head.
Layout utilities use concise single-purpose classes for stacks, rows, spacers, colors, and monospace text.
Comments document intended usage and visual role directly above related rules.
Spacing, type, and color values are mostly tokenized, while a few dimensions and rgba values are literal.
concerns: .cv-bullets--tight > li sets gap on list items, but those list items are not declared as flex containers in this file.
The watermark comment says octagon, while the CSS draws a circular bordered mark with border-radius: 50%.
The .cv-pill rule uses cursor: pointer for the base pill, which may imply interactivity even when the class is used as a static label.
transition: all on .cv-pill can animate properties beyond the explicit visual states defined here.
notes: This is a token-driven ConceptV deck primitive stylesheet that packages recurring visual language into reusable cv- classes.
questions: Should .cv-watermark be circular as implemented, or octagonal as the section comment says?
Was .cv-bullets--tight intended to reduce the list container gap rather than set gap on each li?
Are resizable pane gutters meant to live in the deck primitive layer or in a component-specific stylesheet?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file provides reusable, on-brand deck building blocks intended to load after token definitions and before canvas-specific styles.
apparent_themes: soft paper and cream surface hierarchy
intention_signals: It seems intended to make repeated ConceptV deck patterns available as stable, reusable CSS classes so different surfaces can share the same visual grammar without recreating slide-specific styling.
novelty: The distinct element is the combination of deck presentation primitives with branded motifs such as gold hatch strips, faint blueprint grids, entity shape sizing, and resizable gutter affordances in one stylesheet.
contribution: This file contributes a reusable visual substrate for ConceptV surfaces: tokenized paper/panel/card hierarchy, branded decorative patterns, deck typography, stats and list treatments, tags, structural comparison patterns, headers, utility classes, and pane gutter styling.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV deck CSS primitive layer defining cv- namespaced surfaces, patterns, typography, stats, lists, pills, tags, rules, watermark, comparison grids, headers, shapes, utilities, and resizable gutters.
intention_for_embedding: This was for preserving the ConceptV pitch deck visual system as reusable building blocks loaded after base color/type tokens and before canvas-specific styles.
